title: gladiolus quadrangularis
intro: Gladiolus quadrangularis, also known as the African corn lily, is a plant species belonging to the Iridaceae family, characterized by its unique, sword-shaped leaves and vibrant flowers. Traditionally, Gladiolus quadrangularis has been used in folk medicine, where its roots are valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of the plant are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Gladiolus quadrangularis is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for use in herbal remedies, with some cultures also using it to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
